chapman_memory_x_citation.note: downward-biased: cap_cite reachability generously defined for well-cited memory papers

three_source_independence.note: OPTIMISTIC CEILING: channels violate independence (era-heterogeneous) and citation-reachability inflates overlap

honest_readout.reason: the three pairwise estimates (142/219/246) disagree, which is diagnostic of catchability heterogeneity across channels — no single N is trustworthy; report the range

saturation_signal: marginal yield falling: memory 80 -> +45 search -> +36 citation, despite citation screening 1154 candidates. Diminishing returns => approaching saturation.
caveats: three heterogeneous channels (memory=old/seminal, search=recent/indexed, citation=well-connected) violate capture-recapture independence
cap_cite for the existing 125 = citation-graph reachability (bwd>=3 or fwd>=3), a generous post-hoc definition that inflates citation overlap
citation candidates excluded prior pools, so citation-only papers cannot be recaptured by memory/search
single membership judge per paper; abstract-availability gaps in OpenAlex; keyless (no Semantic Scholar / PubMed)
saturation_verdict: Four decorrelated channels; last two added +36 and +0 in-scope against 1154 and 263 screened candidates. Marginal yield collapsed => corpus saturating at 161. The embedding null (orthogonal, seed-centered) argues few un-caught in-scope papers remain near the corpus; discounted for D's recency bias (old stratum covered by citation channel).
